data.body:
**Completion verification — PASS**

All three acceptance criteria are met by the implementation.

---

**AC 1 — Overlays fire only on relevant plans; not-applicable returns no finding (near-zero false-fire)**

MET. The orchestrator (`src/rebar/llm/plan_review/orchestrator.py:184–188`) skips any deterministic overlay whose trigger did not fire. LLM-routed overlays enter the finder but every T-overlay prompt (e.g. `plan_review_T10.md`, `plan_review_T11.md`, `plan_review_T12.md`) opens with "PASS not-applicable" as the baseline. The `applies_at` gating in `registry.applies()` (`registry.py:214–240`) further suppresses overlays outside their declared level/type scope. Eval corpus run (`docs/experiments/plan-review-gate/runs/e4_suite.jsonl`) and the E3 analysis script (`harnesses/analyze_e4.py:97–110`) confirm false-fire measurement is in place and results are recorded.

**AC 2 — Deterministic vs LLM routing per the registry trigger field**

MET. Every T-overlay entry in `criteria_routing.json` carries an `"overlay_routing"` field (`"deterministic"` or `"llm"`). The registry exposes it via `_descriptor_from_prompt` (`registry.py:165`). Deterministic triggers are implemented in `_DET_OVERLAY_RULES` (`registry.py:275–280`) for the low-FP set (T5a, T5d, T7, T12-keyword). The Scope requirement — T10/T11/T12 LLM-routed for plans — is confirmed by `criteria_routing.json` lines 1057, 1100, 1135 (`"overlay_routing": "llm"`) and matches the experimental rationale (E4-confirmed: deterministic keyword over-fires on 'migration'/'deploy' in plan prose).

**AC 3 — Trigger precision measured on the eval corpus**

MET. The E4 harness (`harnesses/e4_singleturn.py`) runs a dedicated TRIGGER job (lines 62–66) comparing `det_overlays()` vs `llm_router()` per ticket and writing `e4_trigger.jsonl`. The analysis script (`analyze_e4.py:97–110`) computes per-overlay det vs LLM fire counts and disagreements. Concrete results are recorded in `docs/experiments/plan-review-finalize-and-threepass.md` (§3): T11 det 2/19 vs LLM 0/19; T12 det 7/19 vs LLM 0/19 — directly confirming the routing decision reflected in the registry.
